Hi Aline. I found two flights for the trip. Let's compare them before I book.
Sure. Option A is the direct flight, so it is faster. But the fare is more expensive, right?
Exactly. Option B is cheaper, but it has a layover in Zurich, so it is longer.
Option B is refundable, and the baggage allowance is bigger. For a work trip, I think it is safer.
Good point. Let's book Option B then. Do you want an aisle seat for the long flight?
Yes, an aisle seat, please. And add travel insurance. Send me the itinerary when it is done.
Let me compare the two flights.
The direct flight is faster but more expensive.
I prefer the refundable fare. Plans can change.
I would like an aisle seat, please.

Hi Aline, this is Tom. I am calling about the trip to the conference in Basel. I found two flight options for us to compare. Option A is a direct flight. It is faster -- only two hours -- but it is more expensive, about four hundred euros. The fare is not refundable, so we cannot change it. Option B is cheaper, about two hundred and fifty euros. It is a round trip with one layover in Zurich, so it is longer -- almost five hours -- but it is refundable, and the baggage allowance is bigger. I would like to book the trip today. Which option do you prefer? Please call me back and let me know. Thanks.

This is a quick voice note to decide on the flight before I call Tom back. Let me compare the two options. Option A is faster, but it is more expensive and the fare is not refundable. Option B is cheaper and refundable, and the baggage allowance is bigger, but the layover in Zurich makes it longer. For a work trip, I think the refundable fare is safer, because our plans can change. I would like the aisle seat so I can move during the long flight. I am going to choose Option B, and I am going to add travel insurance to be safe. I will call Tom and confirm the itinerary today.

First, Tom calls to plan the trip to the Basel conference. Then he explains that there are two flight options to compare. After that, he describes Option A, which is faster but more expensive. Next, Aline compares the fares and decides that the cheaper, refundable option is safer. Finally, she chooses Option B, adds travel insurance, and confirms the itinerary.
